claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 7 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 7 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit! Let me start the assessment process.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's begin. The first thing I need to know is about the child or children you're claiming for.

**How old is your child?** (Or if you have multiple children, let's start with one child at a time)

Please tell me:
- The child's name (or just "Child 1" if you prefer)
- Their age

This will help me determine which eligibility path applies to them.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Hello! Yes, I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit and wanted to check if I'm eligible.

I have one child - Alex, who is 7 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! Let me help you check your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Since Alex is 7 years old (under 16), that's a good start. Now I need to check a few more things.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Is Alex working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?**

At 7 years old, I'm assuming Alex is not in paid employment, but I need to confirm this to continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't work at all - he's only 7!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! That makes sense. Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Is Alex NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right?**

In other words, is Alex receiving any benefits like Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit in his own name? 

Again, at age 7 this would be very unusual, but I need to confirm.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't receive any benefits in his own name.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Is Alex in any type of apprenticeship?**

Since Alex is only 7 years old, I'm assuming he's not in an apprenticeship program, but I need to confirm this to move forward.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in an apprenticeship - he's just 7 years old!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Of course! Thank you for bearing with me through these questions. Now let me move on to questions about you as the claimant.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Now I need to check your residency status. 

**Do you meet the UK residency requirements?**

You'll qualify if ANY of the following apply to you:

1. **You normally live in the UK** (this is the most common situation)
2. You have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. You have pre-settled status and are working (earning above National Insurance threshold)
4. You have pre-settled status and are actively seeking work (up to 91 days)
5. You have pre-settled status with sufficient resources to support yourself
6. You have pre-settled status and are studying with sufficient resources
7. You have pre-settled status as a family member of an EEA/Swiss national
8. You're living abroad as a Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

**Which of these applies to your situation?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I don't actually live in the UK - I'm living abroad at the moment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I see. Since you're living abroad, I need to check if you meet any of the special circumstances that would still allow you to claim Child Benefit.

**Can you tell me more about your situation abroad?**

Specifically:
- **Are you a Crown servant** (e.g., working for the UK government, diplomatic service, armed forces)?
- **Or are you living in certain qualifying countries** with a connection to UK employment or service?

If neither of these applies, then unfortunately living abroad would make you ineligible for Child Benefit, as it requires you to normally be resident in the UK or meet one of these special exceptions.

What's your situation?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not a Crown servant or anything like that. I'm just living abroad with my family.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for clarifying. Based on the information you've provided, I now have enough to make a determination about your Child Benefit eligibility.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Child Benefit Eligibility Assessment - Result

I'm sorry to inform you that **you are not eligible for Child Benefit** at this time.

### Summary for Alex (age 7):

**Result: INELIGIBLE**

**Reason:** While Alex meets all the child-related requirements (he's under 16, not working, not receiving benefits in his own right, and not in an apprenticeship), you do not meet the UK residency requirements necessary to claim Child Benefit.

### Why you're ineligible:

To claim Child Benefit, you must:
- Normally live in the UK, **OR**
- Meet special circumstances such as:
  - Having settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme (with qualifying criteria)
  - Being a Crown servant working abroad
  - Living in certain qualifying countries with a connection to UK employment or service

Since you're living abroad and none of these special circumstances apply to your situation, you cannot claim Child Benefit.

### What you should know:

If your circumstances change and you return to live in the UK, you would be able to claim Child Benefit at that time, as Alex meets all the child-related eligibility criteria.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit or your situation?

reasonings: Alex is 7 years old, which means he qualifies as a child under 16. He is not in paid work, does not receive benefits in his own right, and is not in an apprenticeship. However, the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements. The claimant is living abroad and is not a Crown servant, does not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, and is not living in qualifying countries with a connection to UK employment or service. Under UK Child Benefit rules, claimants must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances to be eligible. Since the claimant does not meet these residency requirements, they are ineligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (age 7), who meets all the child-related eligibility criteria. However, the claimant is living abroad and does not meet any of the UK residency requirements or special circumstances exceptions. Therefore, the claimant is ineligible for Child Benefit.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.reason: Does not meet UK residency requirements
response.guidance: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances (settled status, pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, Crown servant, or living in certain countries). If you have pre-settled status, you may need to be working, studying, or have sufficient resources.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 7 years old, which means he qualifies as a child under 16. He is not in paid work, does not receive benefits in his own right, and is not in an apprenticeship. However, the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements. The claimant is living abroad and is not a Crown servant, does not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, and is not living in qualifying countries with a connection to UK employment or service. Under UK Child Benefit rules, claimants must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances to be eligible. Since the claimant does not meet these residency requirements, they are ineligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (age 7), who meets all the child-related eligibility criteria. However, the claimant is living abroad and does not meet any of the UK residency requirements or special circumstances exceptions. Therefore, the claimant is ineligible for Child Benefit.
